args: I like long-duration growth equities here (high-multiple software, unprofitable-but-scaling tech) because the rate-cutting cycle is starting. Lower discount rates disproportionately re-rate long-duration cash flows, and growth has historically outperformed value in the first 6-12 months after the first cut. Inflation has trended down three straight quarters, so the path of least resistance for rates is down. This is a sector/style rotation, not a single-name pick.
note: VARIANCE ANCHOR (run x3). Pass-1-only, no tickers -> Position-Level Read omitted; canonical macro/theme/implicit case. Watch: Assumption Strength light, hype reading, load-bearing ranking stability across runs.

args: I want to buy TSLA.O because it's been going up and everyone I follow is bullish, Elon is a genius, the stock always comes back, and robotaxis are coming soon. I think it could double.
note: PUSH-BACK case. Deliberately weak -> confirmation-bias + narrative-fallacy; skill must flag, hype meter should read High/red, not rubber-stamp.

args: I'm short USD/JPY -- long yen, short dollar -- because the BoJ is normalizing policy while the Fed is about to cut, so the rate differential compresses and the yen rallies over the next two quarters. This is a pure macro FX / carry-unwind trade.
note: COVERAGE case. Pure FX -> Coverage Notice must read 'partial' (country-regime read via currency/fixed_income, NOT the USD/JPY instrument); must not fabricate a pair-level read.

args: Bitcoin is heading higher post-halving with ETF inflows structurally bid and supply issuance halved, so I'm going heavy into BTC directly, plus MSTR.O as a leveraged equity proxy for the same trade.
note: COVERAGE case. BTC out-of-scope as an instrument -> reachable only via the MSTR.O equity expression (tested as layer-3). Coverage Notice must say so up front.

args: I like long-duration growth equities here because the rate-cutting cycle is starting, lower discount rates re-rate long-duration cash flows, and growth outperforms value in the first 6-12 months after the first cut. Style rotation, not a single-name pick.
note: TWO-PASS case. Same Pass-1 as macro_led_long; retiree drawing income, low capacity, concentrated -> Pass 2 must re-weight severity (sequence-of-returns), keep Pass-1 statuses identical, and render the stronger disclaimer variant.
